title: rbac — retention windows
description: Role + permission rows retained while tenant active. Pivots (role_has_permissions + model_has_roles + model_has_permissions) cascade on parent delete. Soft-deleted custom roles retained 90 days for restore, then hard-purged. RoleDefinition never deleted — only marked is_active=false. Audit rows in the audit module have their own retention window.
compliance_regimes_referenced: gdpr Art. 5(1)(e) (storage limitation), Art. 17 (right to erasure)

hot.days: permanent while catalogue references the row

purge.trigger: manual archive by platform admin (super_admin) + zero active model_has_roles references
purge.notes: System roles are shipped by Stackra. Removing one is a platform decision — usually happens when a business_type is deprecated.

hot.days: while soft-delete window open (up to 90 days after deleted_at)

purge.notes: 90-day soft-delete window supports admin recovery ('oops, I deleted the wrong role'). Beyond 90 days, hard-purge.

purge.trigger: removed from PermissionCatalogueLoader (i.e. no module #[ProvidesPermissions] contributes this name)
purge.notes: Never hard-purged because historic audit rows may reference the permission name. Hard-purge would break audit read-back.
Tenant-scoped custom permissions have no cross-tenant audit value — hard-cascade on tenant delete.

hot.days: while parent role AND parent permission both live

purge.trigger: role hard-delete OR permission hard-delete OR explicit detach
expires_at reached (fires RoleAssignmentExpired) OR explicit revoke OR principal delete OR role delete
purge.notes: Assignment history is preserved in the audit-log stream (owned by the audit module) — not in this table. Revoked/expired assignments are hard-purged; the audit row remembers.

purge.trigger: explicit revoke OR principal delete OR permission delete

hot.days: permanent — never deleted, only marked is_active=false

purge.notes: Role definitions are shipped catalogue entries. Removed entries are marked is_active=false so seeder loops skip them but historic tenants can still reference them. Hard purge only happens as part of a controlled platform migration.
